primary_recommendation.rationale: Only dataset satisfying all 4 immediate criteria: downloaded, real default labels, real demographics, no auth barrier
primary_recommendation.citation: Yeh, I.C. and Lien, C.H. (2009). The comparisons of data mining techniques for the predictive accuracy of probability of default of credit card clients. Expert Systems with Applications, 36(2), 2473-2480.
known_limitations: Credit card default, not consumer loan default — different product
Taiwan market, 2005 data — not representative of current credit populations
No loan amount / outstanding principal (installment loan features absent)
EDUCATION/MARRIAGE codes have undocumented categories (0, 5, 6 in EDUCATION; 0 in MARRIAGE)

dataset_decisions.decision_rationale: Ideal match for synthetic DGP; enables direct SR-1 through SR-7 reproduction; blocked by Kaggle auth in automated environment
Primary value is rejected loan pool for reject inference; 27M rejected rows; blocked by Kaggle auth
No default outcome — approval/denial only; mandatory demographic fields; regulatory-grade fairness lane; not for champion/challenger
Real credit performance; no demographic variables; primary source offline; too small for drift experiments
Real US consumer default labels; only age as demographic; Kaggle auth; simpler than Home Credit
Downloaded, verified, real default labels, real demographics (SEX/AGE/EDUCATION/MARRIAGE), temporal panel, no auth
1,000 rows; 1994 data; 30% default rate unrealistic; recognized as toy dataset; interview-damaging if cited as primary

synthetic_harness_lane.role: Injected failure testing; controlled leakage/drift/calibration protocol
synthetic_harness_lane.rationale: Cannot remove synthetic harness — it is the only environment with injected temporal leakage, controlled DGP drift, and known Bayes ceiling

real_data_immediate_lane.role: Business-realistic champion/challenger evaluation; real demographic fairness audit

real_data_future_lane.role: Gold standard; source-reference reproduction; Kaggle — user manual download required

pending_decision_before_g6.question: Which data lane should G6 Champion/Challenger run on?

options.pro: Clean real-data story; forces pipeline to handle real data
options.con: Loses direct comparison with synthetic protocol results

options.description: Synthetic + Taiwan parallel lanes (run G6 twice)
options.pro: Full comparison; validates pipeline portability across datasets
options.con: Double the work; requires managing two evidence sets

options.description: Synthetic retained for failure tests; Taiwan Default becomes primary G6 evidence
options.pro: Best portfolio story; demonstrates lane separation explicitly
options.con: Slightly more complex to document clearly

pending_decision_before_g6.recommended_rationale: Option C gives the strongest interview narrative: synthetic lane tests protocol correctness (injected failure detection); Taiwan Default lane tests business-realistic modeling (real data, real demographics). The two lanes are complementary, not redundant.

claim_boundary_note: No performance claim may use Taiwan results as substitute for Home Credit or synthetic DGP results. Taiwan credit card default (Taiwan, 2005) is a separate dataset with different product, geography, and vintage. Claims must be labeled: 'Taiwan credit card default dataset' — not 'Home Credit' and not 'consumer loan'.
